headline: AI Chip Capacity Shifts, Geopolitical Tensions Drive Oil, Big Tech Earnings Eyed
summary: Geopolitical tensions escalated, pushing oil prices above $90/barrel and impacting futures. AI chip supply chains are seeing shifts, with Google exploring Samsung for next-gen TPU production amid TSMC capacity constraints. Investors are closely watching upcoming Big Tech earnings for insights into AI infrastructure spending and broader market sentiment.

bullets.lead: Google is diversifying its next-gen AI chip production.

segments.text: Google is reportedly considering Samsung for the production of memory I/O dies for its 10th-gen TPU 'Icefish' using a 2nm process, while TSMC handles the core 1.4nm compute engines, driven by TSMC's tight capacity and Google's internal compute constraints.

bullets.lead: SK Hynix is accelerating US semiconductor investments.

segments.text: The company is increasing US investments due to soaring prices for next-gen memory, with its ADRs and local shares becoming convertible on July 29, potentially opening arbitrage opportunities.

sources.title: 차세대 메모리 단가 폭등에 GPU 제동…SK, 美 반도체 투자 속도

bullets.lead: Optical interconnect demand is surging for AI data centers.

segments.text: Goldman Sachs raised Innolight's price target on higher silicon photonics module volumes and increased AI capex, while Ciena, Lumentum, and VIAVI are positioned for growth in the optical buildout for AI data centers.

sources.title: Silicon Photonics Investment Is Ramping Fast as AI Clusters Outgrow Copper Wiring. 2 AI Stocks Stand to Win.

segments.text: The foundry giant exceeded revenue, gross margin, operating margin, net profit, and EPS guidance, with revenue up 33.7% YoY.

bullets.lead: US-Iran tensions escalated, driving oil prices higher.

segments.text: Iran's supreme leader accused Trump of violating a deal and vowed a forceful response, contributing to Dow Jones Futures falling and oil prices crossing $90/barrel as the Middle East conflict widened, leading to suspended oil loadings from the Caspian Pipeline Consortium.

bullets.lead: Major tech companies are expanding their presence in South Korea.

segments.text: Microsoft and Amazon are reportedly choosing Korean provinces for new investments, bypassing China and Japan, while ASML supplier Trumpf noted South Korea's EUV demand is growing faster than Taiwan's.

x_themes.topic: Kimi K3's Performance, Economic Impact, and Implications for AI Model Competition
x_themes.summary: The release of Kimi K3, a 2.8 trillion parameter open-source model, has sparked debate on AI model economics; while its token inefficiency makes it expensive per task, it demonstrates China's frontier compute capability, validates scaling laws, and intensifies competition, potentially benefiting AI infrastructure by lowering model layer margins.

x_themes.topic: Market Correction Driven by Deleveraging & Liquidation Cascades
x_themes.summary: The market is experiencing a significant correction, particularly in beta/momentum stocks, driven by unprecedented leverage, widespread margin calls, and forced liquidations in the Korean market and broader AI-related assets, leading some to anticipate generational buying opportunities.

x_themes.topic: AI Datacenter Optical Interconnect Layers & InP Light Source
x_themes.summary: PhotonCap details the three layers of AI datacenter optical interconnect (in-rack, intra-datacenter, inter-datacenter) and highlights the common InP light source across all, noting META's Prometheus project extends inter-datacenter links up to 2,000 km.

x_themes.topic: Ciena's Role in AI Datacenter Interconnect & Hyper-Rail
x_themes.summary: Crux Capital details Ciena's strong position in connecting AI data centers across vast distances, driven by demand for coherent optics and DCI, with Hyper-Rail poised as a major future growth driver. Ciena has secured a hyperscaler order for Hyper-Rail, with ramp starting in 2027.

x_themes.topic: Lumentum's Growth in Optical Buildout & 1.6T Transceivers
x_themes.summary: Crux Capital highlights Lumentum's strong position in the optical buildout, driven by demand for 800G/1.6T transceivers, OCS, and CPO external light sources, with 1.6T modules expected to double ASP and demand exceeding supply.

x_themes.topic: Goldman Sachs Raises Innolight PT on Strong Optical Demand
x_themes.summary: Goldman Sachs significantly raised Innolight's price target and future earnings estimates, citing much higher silicon photonics module volumes, increased AI capex, and strong demand for 1.6T/3.2T modules, indicating robust growth in the optical sector.

x_themes.topic: VIAVI's Role in Optical Buildout Testing & Increased Test Intensity
x_themes.summary: Crux Capital highlights VIAVI's critical role in testing the optical buildout, covering various datacenter components from silicon photonics to physical fiber networks, noting that increasing complexity in technologies like CPO and advanced packaging drives higher test intensity and demand.

x_themes.topic: Memory Stock Decline Driven by Deleveraging and Future Supply Expansion
x_themes.summary: Jukan05 presents an analysis suggesting the decline in memory stocks is due to unwinding leveraged positions and the market pricing in future supply expansion from Samsung and SK Hynix, expected to ease supply-demand imbalance by 2028.

x_themes.topic: US ITC Section 337 Investigation into DRAM Devices
x_themes.summary: FundaAI reports on the U.S. International Trade Commission's Section 337 investigation into certain dynamic random-access memory devices, which could impact Samsung and the broader DRAM industry.

x_themes.topic: SK Hynix ADR-Local Share Convertibility & Arbitrage
x_themes.summary: Aleabitoreddit notes that SK Hynix ADRs and local shares become convertible on July 29, which is expected to open arbitrage opportunities and likely compress the current 25%+ ADR premium.

x_themes.topic: Google's Internal AI Compute Capacity Constraints
x_themes.summary: KawzInvests reports that Google engineers are facing internal AI compute capacity constraints, with Gemini 3.5 Pro behind schedule, despite the company's massive $180-190 billion capex guidance and Q1 capex doubling year-over-year.

x_themes.topic: Nebius Raises $775M Debt to Accelerate Growth
x_themes.summary: Nebius successfully raised $775 million in oversubscribed debt at attractive terms (SOFR + 2.50%), signaling institutional confidence as it continues to deliver planned capacity tranches to Microsoft.

x_themes.topic: Meta in Talks to Lease Compute to Anthropic ($10B Deal)
x_themes.summary: Meta is reportedly in talks to lease compute capacity to Anthropic in a $10 billion deal, which further validates the business models of neocloud providers.
TSMC reported strong Q2 2026 earnings, beating revenue, gross margin, operating margin, net profit, and EPS guidance, with revenue up 33.7% YoY.
